message:
Add Ratatui TUI infrastructure scaffolding

Establish ui module structure with placeholder files for Ratatui-based
terminal user interface implementation: main TUI loop, widget components,
and theming system.

Module purpose (per PLAN § 3.1, § 6):
The ui layer provides the interactive terminal interface for search input,
results browsing, and conversation detail viewing using Ratatui (modern
tui-rs fork) with crossterm backend for cross-platform terminal control
(Windows/Linux/macOS support without platform-specific code paths).

Planned tui.rs responsibilities (PLAN § 6.1-6.3):

Event loop structure:
- Initialize crossterm terminal: enable raw mode (disable line buffering,
  capture Ctrl+C), enter alternate screen (preserve user's shell scrollback),
  enable mouse capture for potential future mouse interaction support
- Create ratatui::Terminal with CrosstermBackend wrapping stdout, set up
  panic hook to restore terminal state on unwrap/panic (critical: if TUI
  panics without restoring, user's terminal left in raw mode rendering
  shell unusable until reset)
- Main loop: poll crossterm events (KeyEvent, MouseEvent, Resize) with
  timeout via crossterm::event::poll(Duration::from_millis(100)) for
  periodic UI refresh (e.g., update indexing progress bar even without
  user input)
- On KeyEvent:
  - '/' key: focus search input box, capture text input until Enter/Esc
  - 'q' key: send shutdown command, break event loop, restore terminal
  - 'f', 'a', 't', 'w' keys: open filter/agent/time/workspace selection
    modals (per PLAN § 6.3 hotkeys)
  - Arrow/vim keys (↑↓jk): move selection in results list, PgUp/PgDn
    for page scrolling, Tab to cycle focus between search/results/detail
    panes
- On tick: check crossbeam channel for UiEvent::SearchResult or
  UiEvent::IndexProgress messages from background threads, update state,
  trigger redraw
- Render: call terminal.draw(|frame| { render_app(frame, &app_state) })
  to rebuild UI via Ratatui widget composition

Layout structure (PLAN § 6.2):
Three-pane vertical split using ratatui::layout::Layout with constraints:
- Top bar (1-2 rows): Paragraph widget for search input with cursor
  position, horizontal Span widgets for filter pills (agent:Codex,
  time:7d), right-aligned status text (e.g., "Indexing... 45%")
- Content area (remaining height): Horizontal split (40%/60% default):
  - Left pane: List widget for search results, each row formatted as
    "[ICON] [TIME] [WORKSPACE] Title snippet" with color_coded style
    based on agent (Codex:cyan, Claude:purple, Gemini:blue, Amp:magenta,
    Cline:green, OpenCode:yellow per PLAN § 6.2 color spec)
  - Right pane: Tabs widget switching between "Messages", "Code Snippets",
    "Raw JSON" tabs, content area shows Paragraph with wrapped text for
    selected conversation's messages or syntax-highlighted snippets
- Bottom status line (1 row): hints like "Enter:open | /:search | f:filters
  | q:quit" using Span widgets with Modifier::DIM

App state management:
Define AppState struct holding:
- search_query: String (current search input text)
- search_results: Vec<ConversationSearchHit> (from Tantivy query)
- selected_result_idx: usize (which conversation highlighted in list)
- active_filters: Filters struct (agent slugs, time range, workspace path)
- focus: FocusPane enum (SearchInput | ResultsList | DetailPane)
- indexing_progress: HashMap<String, (usize, usize)> (per-agent current/total)
- theme: Theme (from config, see theme.rs below)

Planned components/widgets.rs responsibilities (PLAN § 6.4):
Custom widget implementations extending Ratatui's built-in widgets:
- SearchInputWidget: Paragraph with cursor rendering (insert "|" at
  cursor position in text), handle character insert/delete/backspace,
  emit SearchQuery events on change for debounced search trigger
- ResultsListWidget: StatefulList wrapper with custom row rendering—
  format ListItem with colored agent icons (→ use Unicode symbols:
  "⚡" Codex, "⚛" Claude, "◆" Gemini, "▲" Amp, "●" Cline, "▣" OpenCode),
  relative timestamps via humantime crate (e.g., "3h ago", "2d ago"),
  highlight style (reverse video + bold) for selected row
- ConversationDetailWidget: Scrollable Paragraph for message rendering,
  role-based prefixes ("You: ", "Agent: ", "Tool: "), timestamp headers,
  horizontal separators between turns (using symbols::line::HORIZONTAL)
- FilterPillWidget: Inline Span cluster showing active filters with
  "[x]" close buttons (future: click to remove filter, for now just
  visual)

Planned components/theme.rs responsibilities (PLAN § 6.4):
Theme system with light/dark variants:
- Theme struct with Color fields: background, foreground, highlight,
  agent_colors: HashMap<String, Color> for per-agent color coding,
  border_style: Style for Block borders, status_dim: Style for hint text
- load_theme(config: &AppConfig) → Theme: read config.general.theme
  string ("dark" | "light"), return corresponding Color constants:
  - Dark theme: background:BLACK, foreground:WHITE, highlight:CYAN,
    agent_colors:{codex:CYAN, claude:MAGENTA, gemini:BLUE, ...}
  - Light theme: background:WHITE, foreground:BLACK, highlight:BLUE,
    agent_colors adjusted for readability on light background
- Apply theme: pass &Theme to all widget render calls, use theme.foreground
  for default Style::fg(), theme.highlight for selected items
- Optional: support custom themes from config.toml with color hex codes
  (parse via ratatui::style::Color::Rgb(r,g,b) from "#rrggbb" strings)

Error handling in TUI context:
Since TUI runs in raw mode, panic messages are invisible (printed to
alternate screen buffer that gets cleared on exit). Install custom panic
hook that restores terminal before panic message display:
```rust
let original_hook = std::panic::take_hook();
std::panic::set_hook(Box::new(move |panic_info| {
    crossterm::terminal::disable_raw_mode().ok();
    crossterm::execute!(std::io::stdout(), crossterm::terminal::LeaveAlternateScreen).ok();
    original_hook(panic_info);
}));
```
For anyhow errors in render loop, catch and display error screen
(full-screen red Paragraph with error.to_string() + backtrace if
available) with "Press 'q' to exit" hint, restore terminal on quit.

Integration with background threads:
TUI thread owns mpsc receiver end of crossbeam channel, background indexer
and search threads send UiEvent messages. In event loop tick handler:
```rust
while let Ok(event) = ui_rx.try_recv() {
    match event {
        UiEvent::SearchResult(results) => {
            app_state.search_results = results;
            app_state.selected_result_idx = 0;
        }
        UiEvent::IndexProgress { agent, current, total } => {
            app_state.indexing_progress.insert(agent, (current, total));
        }
        _ => {}
    }
}
```
Render function reads app_state to display latest results and progress
without blocking on channel receive (non-blocking try_recv).

This commit contains only module declarations (mod.rs, tui.rs,
components/mod.rs, components/widgets.rs, components/theme.rs) with
placeholder comments to establish the ui/ namespace per implementation
checklist (PLAN § 14.3: minimal TUI with dummy data after core modules,
before connectors). Actual Ratatui widget composition, event loop, and
crossterm terminal setup will be implemented in subsequent commit to
demonstrate end-to-end UI flow with hardcoded search results before
wiring storage/search/indexer.

Related: PLAN_TO_MAKE_CODING_AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH.md § 3.1 (UI component),
§ 3.2 (threading model), § 6 (TUI design), Cargo.toml (ratatui, crossterm
dependencies), README.md (ui/ module description).
